title: Double Descent, Made Concrete
tagline: More parameters than data points should overfit. They sometimes generalize better instead.
summary: Random-feature ridge regression on a 1-D curve, with the model size knob exposed. Watch the test error rise into the U, peak around the interpolation threshold, then descend again as the model gets even bigger — the double-descent phenomenon, in your browser.
highlights: Live random-feature regression with a model-size slider; test error draws the double-descent curve.
Slide ridge λ to suppress the spike — overparameterised regimes are sensitive to regularisation.
Same dataset, different noise levels; see how noise shifts the spike location and height.
